purpose: hci_cmd_sync_queue is changed to return an error when the HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set so commands are not queued after hci_unregister_dev may free hdev, preventing a use-after-free triggered by later timeout handling.

Remove an unnecessary include of asm/termios.h from n_hdlc.c because linux/tty.h (already included) pulls linux/termios.h; simplifies include graph and removes an outlier include that complicates termios header handling.
Updates perf c2c documentation to reflect the addition of an 'N/A' entry for store operations in the report table, clarifying how the new metric appears in perf outputs.

Fixes hugetlb vma-lock handling: allocate a new vma lock when vm_area_dup() splits a VMA to avoid double-free, free the vma lock instead of clearing VM_MAYSHARE to avoid missed frees, and update pmd-sharing checks to eliminate races during split and range unmapping.

Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply properties to the realtek rt5682s device-tree binding, documenting the codec's power rails already used by sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi so platforms can describe and validate these supplies in DT.
Adds the missing iounmap() call to zorro7xx_remove_one() to unmap an ioremap region that the probe's error path may allocate, closing a resource leak during device removal.
Removes the unused 'work' field from struct mlx5e_async_ctx in the mlx5e kTLS code; a small structural cleanup to eliminate dead/unused member and simplify the code.
Change cfg80211_auth_request.key_idx from unsigned to signed so it can hold -1 as a sentinel when key is NULL, avoiding static-checker warnings and matching the intended value range (-1..3).
Reorders the CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS entries in RISC-V defconfigs to match savedefconfig ordering; purely a cosmetic/consistency change in configuration files with no functional impact.
Introduce mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() helper and replace scattered inline checks with this function to centralize detection of whether the chip FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode.
Change updates RTC selftests to detect absence of RTC hardware and skip tests instead of failing, making the test suite tolerant of platforms without an RTC.
Removes a redundant assignment to the 'bytes' variable in echoaudio's midi.c (dead store), eliminating a Clang static-analysis warning and cleaning up unused code without changing runtime behavior.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks so rcuscale can be built without pulling in RCU Tasks under CONFIG_PREEMPT_NONE, reducing unnecessary kernel size and tracing complexity.
Fixes a duplicated word in spte.h comment ('be split be split' → 'be split'), a trivial documentation typo correction in KVM x86 MMU header.

Reverts the change that populated the DCN315 clock table from dcfclk states because, when pmfw disables dcfclk states, that approach can cause a 'no read' underflow. The revert restores the previous behavior until a proper solution is implemented.
Adds a device-tree binding entry (compatible string) for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC that uses a Bifrost Mali‑G31 GPU, documenting the SoC in the mali‑bifrost bindings so platforms can describe this GPU.
Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp in the DesignWare PCIe core to follow the driver's naming convention (dw_ prefix, _rp suffix), making type names consistent with other driver entities.

Corrects a printk format in amd-xgbe: the u16 argument (ntohs(eth->h_proto)) is promoted to int, so the format is changed to %x to eliminate a Clang -Wformat warning and follow printk-format guidance.

Change casts the upper bound of a branch-range check to long so the comparison is signed, avoiding a compiler warning when a negative offset is encountered and clarifying the intended semantics in MIPS jump_label compat handling.
Removes the HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU and calls the underlying function directly as part of removing the HAL layer, simplifying control flow in the staging r8188eu driver.
Marks previously global symbols as static in arm64 errata code (e.g., cavium_erratum_23154_cpus) to silence sparse warnings and limit symbol visibility after adding errata.

Updates the display/msm device-tree bindings to remove an incorrect interrupt flags cell from MDSS consumer nodes because the MDSS interrupt provider is single-cell; aligns binding with provider encoding to prevent invalid DT descriptions.
Modifies media v4l2-ctrls to allocate space for arrays inside struct ctrl_ref (and allocate arrays), preparing the code to allow drivers to change array sizes dynamically.

Extends the Qualcomm socinfo driver by adding entries for additional SoCs (SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P, another SM8450) and associated PMICs so the driver recognizes these platforms and their power-management ICs.
Extend rtl8xxxu driver reporting to recognize chip 'cut' versions A..P (0..15) instead of treating values > E as 'unknown', improving hardware identification and log output.

Remove the custom .is_enabled software enable count from ux500 PRCMU clocks because the clock core already maintains an enable count; this eliminates redundant state and makes the driver rely on core bookkeeping.

Reorganizes RISC-V mm/init code to reduce scattered #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage by grouping related XIP-only declarations and moving the pt_ops macro adjacent to the XIP conditional, simplifying conditional compilation and layout.
Updates the arm64 device tree for Qualcomm sm6350-lena to enable QUP and GPI DMA hardware blocks so functioning I2C nodes can be added and used later.

Clarifies and corrects comments in the rzg2l thermal driver: replaces 'Capture times' with 'Total number of ADC data samples', fixes typo 'caliberation'→'calibration', and adjusts comment placement to match coding style.

Change modifies igc release_swfw_sync to detect failure acquiring the hardware semaphore (e.g., on surprise removal), log an error and exit instead of looping indefinitely, preventing a hang when HW is gone.
Replaces legacy ASoC DAI bus-clock/style defines in the pcm3168a driver with modern ASoC DAI format terminology, aligning the driver with current kernel conventions and removing deprecated macros.
Removes an always-true IS_SWAPFILE conditional/hack from the NFS codebase because IS_SWAPFILE is always defined, eliminating dead/pointless code and simplifying the logic around swapfile handling.

Implements GTP echo-response handling: on receiving GTP_ECHO_REQ the kernel sends GTP_ECHO_RSP including a GTPIE_RECOVERY element and appropriate restart counter (0 for v1, configurable for v0), ensuring compliance when no userspace daemon is present.
Replaces uses of cpumask_next_* with cpumask_first_* where iteration starts at 0 (n == -1) for simplicity/efficiency and adds cpumask_first_zero() to support this pattern.

Remove brcmfmac's dtim_assoc IOVAR query that firmware no longer supports; stops the driver attempting a request that always fails after STA roam, eliminating repeated -52 console errors and unnecessary firmware queries.

Adds an ASoC board configuration (adl_rt5682) to support the ALC5682 headset codec on platforms without a speaker amplifier, wiring the codec to SSP0 per Intel BT-offload design so the board can function without an external amp.
Modify io_uring net path so zerocopy sendmsg is rejected when the socket/protocol doesn't support zerocopy, mirroring prior behavior for zerocopy send requests and preventing unsupported submissions.

Modify kerneldoc preamble to test for xeCJK.sty before attempting to load it, avoiding xelatex 'xeCJK.sty not found' errors on minimal/fragmented texlive installs and allowing 'make pdfdocs' for non‑CJK content on such systems.

Modify scripts/checkpatch.pl to accept 'case' macros (e.g. '#define C2S(x) case x: return #x') by not flagging them as 'Macros with complex values', avoiding false-positive lint errors for existing kernel drivers.

Replaces simple-KMS helpers in simpledrm with the standard DRM atomic helpers (including removing empty CRTC enable/disable and always running drm_atomic_helper_check_plane_state()), improving architecture and code sharing without changing functionality.
Adds a JPEG decode worker interface in the MediaTek mtk-jpegdec driver to coordinate three hardware decoders to run in parallel on MT8195, enabling concurrent decoding and higher throughput.
Removes redundant VPRINTK() calls and the ata_scsi_dump_cdb() helper from libata, relying on existing tracepoints for the same information to reduce duplicate/noisy debug output and simplify logging.

Implements logic to allow entering zstate during extended vblank when FREESYNC_STATE_VIDEO is entered: computes extra vblank time, updates min_dst_y_next_start, and triggers full stream updates to enable zstate if gained time >5 ms.

Changes the hqd_destroy() callback prototype so its third parameter is uint32_t (matching implementations that used enum kfd_preempt_type), eliminating a kCFI type mismatch observed during boot on some AMD GPUs.

Corrects the device-tree binding for stm32 pinctrl: rename the reset property to 'resets' (the correct binding key) and add 'additionalProperties: false' to the schema so unexpected properties are rejected, aligning the binding with actual usage and improving validation.
Downgrades/silences a spurious GCC 12 array-bounds warning in ath9k/mac.c (ath9k_hw_resettxqueue) by reducing the warning level, removing noisy build warnings without changing runtime behavior.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in the sun8i-ss crypto driver, simplifying runtime-PM handling so the device is resumed and usage-counted with the more appropriate API.

Extracts fixed-file installation/removal from io_kiocb helpers into the filetable area and changes the helpers to accept a ring-context (ctx) rather than an io_kiocb, improving ownership and API placement.

Update NXP imx8mq VPU device-tree bindings to split G1 and G2 into separate nodes because they are independent decoder blocks controlled by the vpu-blk-ctrl power-domain; enables accurate DT descriptions and requires kernel support for the new layout.
Remove the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro by inlining its single use into IWL_DEVICE_BZ, simplifying iwlwifi configuration macros and reducing indirection in the codebase.
